---
format: 
  html:
    link-external-icon: true
    link-external-newwindow: true
from: markdown+autolink_bare_uris
number-depth: 2
toc-depth: 2
date: last-modified
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
---

# Welcome {.unnumbered}

Welcome to the **OUCRU Introduction to Medical Statistics 2026**. This course is designed to provide clinicians and researchers with the fundamental statistical tools required for medical research.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## 📅 Logistics & Schedule
-   **Time:** 09:15 – 16:45 (UTC +7) daily
-   **Lunch Break:** 12:30 – 13:30 (UTC +7)
-   **Full Schedule:** [Download Timetable](handouts/Agenda_StatCourse_23-27March2026.pdf)
-   *Note: Lunch is provided for in-person attendees.*
:::

## Contributors {.unnumbered}

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
-   **Prof. Ronald Geskus**
-   **Dr. Duc Du Hong**
-   **Dr. Hoang Van Thuan**
-   **Dr. Nguyen Lam Vuong**
-   **Dr. Tran Thai Hung**
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Pre-course Setup

::: callout-tip
## 🛠️ Choose Your Environment

You have two ways to follow the course exercises:

1.  **Zero Setup (Recommended):** Use the [**Web-R**]{.badge .bg-primary} versions directly in your browser.
2.  **Local Setup:** If you prefer using **RStudio**, please complete the [Pre-course Setup Handout](handouts/precourse_setup.qmd){target="_blank"} before joining.
:::

# Course Topics

## Day 1: Descriptive and Exploratory Data Analysis

### 🧑‍🏫 Lead instructors: Ronald


### ☀️ Morning: Descriptive Analysis

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
#### Contents

1.  Data characteristics & Variable types
2.  Numerical & Graphical summaries
3.  Intro to R and RStudio

#### Materials

-   **Lecture:** [Slides](slides/day_1/1.SlidesDescriptives.pdf)
-   **Exercises:**
    -   [Web-R version](handouts/day_1/Exercise1_Webr.html){target="_blank"}
    -   [RStudio version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseRStudio.html){target="_blank"} ([Script](handouts/day_1/ExerciseCode.R))
-   **Answers:**
<div id="morning-answers">
  <button onclick="unlockMorning1()" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm">Click to unlock answers</button>
</div>


:::


### 🌤️ Afternoon: Exploratory Analysis

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
#### Contents

1.  Baseline tables
2.  Graphs for categorical variables
3.  Numeric variables by group
4.  2-variable numeric plots

#### Materials

-   **Lecture:** [Slides](slides/day_1/2.SlidesEDA.pdf)
-   **Exercises:**
    -   [Web-R version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseWebr-2.html){target="_blank"}
    -   [RStudio version](handouts/day_1/ExerciseRStudio-2.html){target="_blank"} ([Script](handouts/day_1/ExerciseCode-2.R))
-   **Answers:**
<div id="afternoon-answers">
  <button onclick="unlockAfternoon1()" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm">Click to unlock answers</button>
</div>


:::




------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Day 3: Comparing continuous variables and Simple linear regression

### 🧑‍🏫 Lead instructors: Vuong


### ☀️ Morning: Comparing continuous variables

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
#### Contents

1.  Student’s t-test vs. Wilcoxon rank-sum test
2.  Paired t-test vs. Wilcoxon signed-rank test

#### Materials

-   **Lecture:** [Slides](slides/day_3/5_Group comparison_Slides.pdf)
-   **Exercises:**
    -   [Web-R version](handouts/day_3/Exercise5_Webr.html){target="_blank"}
-   **Answers:**
<div id="morning-answers">
  <button onclick="unlockSession('TooEasy?', 'morning-answers', './handouts/day_3/Exercise5_Answers.html')">Unlock Morning</button>
</div>


:::


### 🌤️ Afternoon:Simple linear regression

::: {layout-ncol="2"}
#### Contents

1.  Visualizing Relationships
2.  The Linear Regression Model
3.  Interpreting Results
4.  Regression Diagnostics

#### Materials

-   **Lecture:** [Slides](slides/day_3/6.simpleLinearRegression_Slides.pdf)
-   **Exercises:**
    -   [Web-R version](handouts/day_3/Exercise6_Webr.html){target="_blank"}
-   **Answers:**
<div id="afternoon-answers">
  <button onclick="unlockSession('RealFun!', 'afternoon-answers', './handouts/day_3/Exercise6_Answers.html')">Unlock Afternoon</button>
</div>


:::










































<script>
function unlockSession(correctPassword, elementId, filePath) {
  var password = prompt("Please enter the session code provided in class:");
  
  if (password === correctPassword) {
    document.getElementById(elementId).innerHTML = 
      '<a href="' + filePath + '" class="btn btn-success btn-sm" target="_blank">Web-R Answers</a>';
  } else if (password !== null) {
    alert("Incorrect code.");
  }
}

</script>
